documents.label: A copy of the minor's birth certificate
A copy of your birth certificate
A court order
Foster care certification
A copy of your driver's license or other government-issued identification
A lawfully executed and valid power of attorney
A copy of the minor's Social Security card
A copy of your Social Security card

authority.notes: Listed under 'To show you are the minor's parent or authorized representative' — any one of these is sufficient.

parentId.notes: Listed under 'For proof of your identity' with 'provide one from each category' — the bullets within this category are OR options.

address.notes: The form does not list a separate proof-of-address document category; the guardian's current address is collected on the form itself.

childId.notes: The form explicitly says 'For the minor, please provide BOTH of the following' — both the minor's Social Security card and birth certificate are required.

documents.label: Child's birth certificate
Proof of guardianship (if not named on child's birth certificate)
Driver's license or another government issued identification card (e.g. state ID card)
Child's Social Security card
Proof of address (bank statement, utility bill, or insurance statement)

authority.notes: The child's birth certificate establishes parentage/authority when the parent is named on it. If the parent is not named on the birth certificate, a separate proof of guardianship (court order) is required instead. These are effectively OR alternatives for proving authority.

childId.notes: The page lists both the child's birth certificate and the child's Social Security card as required documents. However, the birth certificate also serves the authority category. Both are listed as separate line items in the 'all of the following' list, suggesting both are required for a complete submission. Marking as 'or' per default guidance, but note the page implies both are expected.

mailingAddress.notes: Page also lists an overnight mail address (701 Experian Parkway, Allen, TX 75013). The standard mail PO Box is the primary mailing address. Note: this page covers checking if a minor has a credit report, not explicitly a security freeze; the address is the same Experian address used for minor-related requests.

documents.label: A document issued by a federal, state, or local government agency showing proof of parentage, including a birth certificate
An order issued by a court of law
Written communication from a county welfare or probation department certifying the protected consumer is in a foster care setting
A guardianship or conservator document
documents.description: A guardianship or conservator document that is current and not expired, includes the protected consumer's name, and that the name of the person on the court document matches the name of the person submitting the request

documents.label: Copy of a driver's license, state-issued identification card, or any other government-issued identification (child)
Copy of a driver's license, state-issued identification card, or any other government-issued identification (parent)
A lawfully executed and valid power of attorney
Social Security number or a copy of a Social Security card (child)
Social Security number or a copy of a Social Security card (parent)

authority.notes: Page explicitly lists these as alternatives ('such as') for 'sufficient proof of authority'. Any one is sufficient. birth_child (child's birth certificate) doubles as both authority (proof of parentage) and childId.

parentId.notes: Step 3 requires 'sufficient proof of identification' for the parent AND the child, but within each person's proof the listed documents are alternatives ('such as'). Any one of these satisfies the parent identity requirement.

address.notes: TransUnion does not require a separate proof-of-address document for the protected consumer freeze. Address proof is only mentioned as optional for standard adult freeze requests.

childId.notes: Step 3 requires 'sufficient proof of identification' for the child. The listed documents are alternatives. birth_child is the same physical document as used for authority (proof of parentage) and is reused here.

mailingAddress.notes: This is the dedicated mailing address explicitly labeled for 'protected consumer freezes' (minor/incapacitated adult freeze). The page also lists P.O. Box 160, Woodlyn, PA 19094 for standard adult freeze requests — that address is NOT for protected consumer freezes.
